# Test: Concurrent Publish (Edge Case)
# Validates that multiple apps publishing to the same canonical module path
# are handled correctly with dry-run.
#
# Prerequisites: k3d and Gitea running (handled by TestE2E)
# Environment: GITEA_URL, GITEA_TOKEN, E2E_CLUSTER are injected by test runner

# Step 1: Create two app repos with same module path
mkdir app1
cd app1
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/shared/v1
exists apx.yaml

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'App1 shared module'
exec git tag proto/shared/v1/v1.0.0
cd ..

mkdir app2
cd app2
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/shared/v1
exists apx.yaml

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'App2 shared module'
exec git tag proto/shared/v1/v1.0.0
cd ..

# Step 2: Both apps attempt to publish to same canonical (dry-run)
cd app1
exec apx publish --module-path=internal/apis/proto/shared/v1 --canonical-repo=github.com/testorg/shared-test --version=v1.0.0 --dry-run
stdout 'Dry-run mode'
cd ..

cd app2
exec apx publish --module-path=internal/apis/proto/shared/v1 --canonical-repo=github.com/testorg/shared-test --version=v1.0.0 --dry-run
stdout 'Dry-run mode'
cd ..

# Step 3: Verify both repos are independent
cd app1
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'App1 shared'
cd ..

cd app2
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'App2 shared'
cd ..

# Success: Concurrent publish scenario validated
# - Multiple apps with same module path can exist independently
# - Both can publish (conflict resolution happens at PR review level)
# - Local state is not corrupted by multiple publish operations
